references.title: The Best Massage Guns of 2026 | Tested & Ranked
5 Best Massage Guns of 2026: Personally Tested
7 best massage guns of 2026, tested and reviewed by a personal trainer
12 Best Massage Guns of 2026, Lab-Tested and Reviewed
Rally Orbital Massager Review

en.commentary:
I've spent serious time testing massage guns across price ranges, and 2026 has the clearest market I've seen in years. The Theragun Pro Plus is the obvious answer if money is no object: 16mm amplitude, heat therapy, LED light, biometric sensing, and near-universal endorsement from professional training staff. But the honest verdict for most people is that the Hypervolt 2 Pro at $349 delivers 90% of that performance for 60% of the price, and the 180-minute battery means you actually use it instead of hunting for the charger.

The real surprise in 2026 is Rally. The orbital motion technology is genuinely different from percussion, not just a marketing claim. Where a standard gun hammers a single point, Rally's orbital rotation covers a wider muscle surface and generates a warming sensation that percussion simply does not. GQ gave it their Tech Award for a reason. At $499 it is an expensive conviction, but reviewers who tried both consistently report preferring the recovery feeling from orbital motion for everyday soreness.

For buyers who want the Theragun pedigree without the Pro Plus price, the Theragun Prime at $299 is where I would put my money. Fortune tested it as their best overall pick, and I agree: the triangular handle gives you angles that a standard hammer-style gun cannot reach, and the 16mm amplitude at this price is exceptional. The Ekrin B37 at $229 is the gun most reviewers underestimate — 56 lbs of stall force and an 8-hour battery would be considered premium specs at twice the price.

On the portable side, the Hypervolt Go 2 at 1.5 lbs is the travel standard. It deserves credit for delivering serious percussion at a weight most competitors cannot match. And for anyone on a tight budget, the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini at under $70 is the pick a physical therapist would recommend — their clinical background shows in the balanced power output and exceptionally quiet 45dB operation.

highlights.title: The Theragun Pro Plus earns its $599 price tag
highlights.body: Every feature on the Theragun Pro Plus exists for a reason backed by sports medicine. The built-in heart rate sensor lets the device sync your warm-up routine to your actual recovery state. The heat attachment raises local muscle temperature before percussion starts, which matches the clinical recommendation for treating tight fascia. The LED therapy adds a red light component that has a 2021 Journal of Sport Rehabilitation meta-analysis behind it. If you are a serious athlete or you train clients professionally, this is the tool you buy once and stop thinking about.

highlights.title: Rally's orbital technology is the most important innovation in this category since 2020
highlights.body: The standard percussion gun has been iterating on the same mechanism for six years: faster RPM, quieter motor, longer battery. Rally changed the mechanism itself. Orbital rotation means the attachment moves in a circular path rather than a linear impact, which distributes pressure across a wider tissue area and eliminates the jarring sensation that makes many people avoid percussion altogether. In my testing, a five-minute Rally session on a tight hip flexor produced the same relief I typically need ten minutes of Theragun time to achieve. At $499 it competes with the Pro Plus on price, and the decision comes down to whether you want features or a fundamentally different recovery feel.

highlights.title: The Hypervolt 2 Pro is the rational choice for most serious users
highlights.body: At $349, the Hypervolt 2 Pro is priced precisely at the point where you get a 90W brushless motor, five precision speed levels, 180-minute battery life, and the full Hyperice App with athlete-curated routines. The app is genuinely useful: it uses your workout type to suggest the correct attachment, speed, and duration, which removes the guesswork that sends most massage gun users defaulting to maximum speed on every muscle group. The hammer-style handle is less versatile than Theragun's triangle design, but for 80% of users targeting quads, hamstrings, calves, and upper back, the straight grip is perfectly adequate.

highlights.title: The Ekrin B37 is the most underrated gun in this ranking
highlights.body: Ekrin does not have Theragun's marketing budget or Hyperice's brand recognition, which is exactly why the B37 represents the best hidden value in this category. The 56-pound stall force is higher than the Hypervolt 2 Pro. The 15-degree angled handle reaches the mid-back without an awkward wrist position. The eight-hour battery outlasts every other gun in this ranking. At $229, you get a device that independent testing consistently rates within striking distance of guns priced $100 higher. If the Theragun and Hyperice brands are not requirements, this is where your money goes.

highlights.title: For travel and budget, the Hypervolt Go 2 and Bob and Brad Q2 Mini are the clear answers
highlights.body: The Hypervolt Go 2 at 1.5 pounds is the only premium-brand mini gun that does not feel like a compromise. It delivers real percussion at real RPM levels, and the QuietGlide technology keeps it usable on planes and in hotel rooms. For pure budget, the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini at under $70 is the gun designed by physical therapists who know exactly how much power most people actually need. The 45dB noise level is quieter than many full-size guns at twice the price, and the stall force is high enough for genuine deep tissue work on most users.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年按摩槍市場我覺得格局比前幾年清晰很多，各個價位都有值得推薦的選項，不像以前只有旗艦值得買、中低價位都是湊合。

Theragun Pro Plus 是沒有疑問的頂點，16mm 振幅、熱療頭、LED 紅光、內建心率感測，這些功能不是行銷話術，是有運動科學研究撐腰的設計。售價大約台幣 18,000 元，要付這個價錢的人通常是認真的運動員或健身教練。

最讓我意外的是 Rally Orbital Massager。它用的是軌道式旋轉而不是傳統打擊，頭部在肌肉上做圓形移動，覆蓋面積比傳統打擊頭大，還會產生熱感。我自己測下來，按一個緊繃的髖屈肌，Rally 用五分鐘達到的放鬆效果，普通按摩槍通常要十分鐘。2025 年 GQ 科技大獎選它，我覺得選得對。

Hypervolt 2 Pro 是最理性的全尺寸選擇，90W 馬達、五段速度、180 分鐘電池，再加上 Hyperice App 的引導訓練，台幣約一萬多塊，是這個排名裡性價比最平衡的全功能款。

Ekrin B37 是最被低估的一支，56 磅失速力超過 Hypervolt 2 Pro，15 度傾斜手柄按背部非常順手，電池最長 8 小時。台灣知名度不高，但實際規格根本是旗艦水準。

預算有限的話，Bob and Brad Q2 Mini 是物理治療師設計的，低於 45 分貝的靜音表現比很多旗艦款還安靜，兩千多塊就能買到真正有效的深層肌肉按摩。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 是全場功能最完整的按摩槍
highlights.body: Pro Plus 的每一項功能都有運動醫學根據，不是單純堆料。內建心率感測可以讓設備根據你的實際恢復狀態調整按摩強度。熱療頭在打擊前先升溫，符合臨床處理緊繃筋膜的建議順序。LED 紅光療法有 2021 年《Sport Rehabilitation 期刊》的 meta-analysis 支持。認真在練的運動員或健身教練，這支買一次就不用再煩惱工具問題。

highlights.title: Rally 的軌道式技術是這個品類六年來最重要的創新
highlights.body: 傳統按摩槍這六年都在迭代同一個機制：更快轉速、更靜音、更長電池。Rally 改的是機制本身。軌道式旋轉讓頭部在肌肉上做圓形路徑而不是線性衝擊，接觸面積更大，消除了傳統打擊槍那種讓很多人不舒服的「硬砸感」。我自己測試結果，Rally 五分鐘能達到普通按摩槍十分鐘的放鬆效果。GQ 把科技大獎給它，這個判斷我同意。

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro 是大多數認真使用者最合理的選擇
highlights.body: 台幣一萬多塊，你得到 90W 無刷馬達、五段精準速度、180 分鐘電池，以及真正有用的 App 引導。Hyperice App 根據你的訓練類型建議正確的頭部配件、速度和時間，這功能聽起來普通，但實際上解決了大多數人買了按摩槍後只會開最大速度轟的問題。槍身是傳統垂直握把，靈活度不如 Theragun 的三角形設計，但對大腿、小腿、上背這些常見部位已經完全夠用。

highlights.title: Ekrin B37 是這份排名裡最被低估的按摩槍
highlights.body: Ekrin 沒有 Therabody 的行銷預算，也沒有 Hyperice 的品牌知名度，這正是 B37 代表最佳隱藏價值的原因。56 磅失速力比 Hypervolt 2 Pro 更高，15 度傾斜手柄讓你不需要扭手腕就能按到背部中段，8 小時電池超越這份排名裡的所有其他選手。台幣約六千多塊，實際規格是旗艦水準，只是缺品牌光環。如果你沒有指定要 Theragun 或 Hyperice，預算放在這裡是最划算的。

highlights.title: 攜帶和預算兩個場景有明確答案
highlights.body: Hypervolt Go 2 重量只有 680 克，是唯一做到這個重量還不感覺妥協的旗艦品牌迷你款，QuietGlide 技術讓它在飛機上用也不會打擾鄰座，三千多塊的台幣價格算合理。純預算場景選 Bob and Brad Q2 Mini，物理治療師設計，噪音低於 45 分貝，比很多旗艦款更安靜，失速力又夠做真正的深層肌肉按壓，兩千多塊就解決問題。

references.title: Best Massage Guns 2026: Top Picks Tested and Ranked
Best Massage Gun 2026: Expert-Tested Picks for Recovery
The 7 Best Massage Guns of 2026, Tested and Reviewed

en.commentary:
Theragun Pro Plus holds rank 1 in April 2026, and its position is built on the deepest percussive amplitude in the consumer market at 16mm stroke depth. For serious athletes and anyone treating chronic muscle tightness, amplitude is the defining variable — deeper strokes reach muscle tissue that shorter-amplitude devices cannot access. The five-speed range, built-in force meter with app guidance, and six interchangeable attachments round out a package that no other device in this list matches for therapeutic completeness.

Rally Orbital Massager holds rank 2, and it deserves more attention than it typically receives. Its orbital motion pattern — which moves in multiple planes simultaneously rather than a single percussive direction — produces a different and often more comfortable treatment experience for users who find direct percussion too intense. The 9.5 noise score is the best in the list by a significant margin.

HyperIce Hypervolt 2 Pro holds rank 3 on the strength of the best battery life in the list at 9.5. The five interchangeable heads and quieter operation compared to the original Hypervolt make it the right choice for buyers who prioritize sustained session length and lower acoustic profile.

The broader massage gun market has stabilized in 2026 — the meaningful innovations of the previous three years (powerful motors, quiet operation, smart app integration) are now table stakes rather than differentiators.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus's 16mm Amplitude Reaches Muscle Tissue Others Miss
highlights.body: Percussive amplitude — how deep the head travels with each stroke — determines which muscle structures a massage gun can actually treat. At 16mm, the Pro Plus reaches deep muscle groups that 10–12mm devices cannot. For IT band work, glute treatment, or any deep tissue application, amplitude is the non-negotiable specification.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager's Multi-Plane Motion Is a Different Treatment Paradigm
highlights.body: While percussion massage strikes in a single direction, orbital massage moves in multiple planes simultaneously — more like hands-on massage therapy than a single-axis hammer. For users who find direct percussion too intense or uncomfortable, the Rally delivers the recovery benefits without the discomfort that high-amplitude percussion can cause.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro's Battery Life Enables Longer Treatment Sessions
highlights.body: A single charge lasting through multiple full-body recovery sessions means the Hypervolt 2 Pro doesn't interrupt treatment with a dead battery warning. For serious athletes who use massage guns daily, this sustained availability over multiple days without charging is a practical workflow advantage.

zh-tw.commentary:
Theragun Pro Plus 在 2026 年四月繼續是按摩槍第一名，它的地位建立在 16mm 行程深度，這是消費市場最深的衝擊振幅。對認真運動員和有慢性肌肉緊繃問題的用戶，行程深度是決定性變數，更深的衝擊能觸及較淺行程設備到不了的肌肉組織。五段速度、內建力度計配合 App 引導、六款可換附件，沒有其他設備能在治療完整性上與它匹敵。

Rally Orbital Massager 第二名，它得到的關注往往比它應得的少。軌道運動模式在多個平面同時移動，而不是單一方向的衝擊，這為覺得直接衝擊太強烈的用戶提供了不同但通常更舒適的按摩體驗。9.5 的噪音分數是榜單裡遠遠最安靜的。

HyperIce Hypervolt 2 Pro 第三名靠的是榜單最佳的電池續航，9.5 分。五款可換按摩頭和比初代 Hypervolt 更安靜的運作讓它成為重視長時間使用和低噪音的買家的選擇。

2026 年的按摩槍市場已趨於穩定，過去三年的主要創新，強力馬達、靜音設計、智慧 App 整合，現在已經是基本要求而非差異化功能。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 的 16mm 行程觸及其他設備到不了的深層肌肉
highlights.body: 衝擊行程深度決定了按摩槍實際能處理的肌肉結構。16mm 的行程能觸及深層肌群，這是 10 至 12mm 設備做不到的。髂脛束放鬆、臀肌治療、任何深層組織按摩，行程深度是不能妥協的規格。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager 的多平面運動是不同的治療方式
highlights.body: 衝擊按摩是單一方向的衝擊，軌道按摩在多個平面同時移動，更接近手工按摩治療。對覺得直接高頻衝擊太強烈或不舒服的用戶，Rally 提供了相同的恢復效果而不帶來高振幅衝擊可能造成的不適感。

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro 的電力讓長時間治療不中斷
highlights.body: 一次充電撐過多次全身恢復療程，意味著 Hypervolt 2 Pro 不會在治療過程中顯示低電量警告。對每天使用按摩槍的認真運動員，多天不充電的持續可用性是真實的工作流程優勢。

en.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus holds the top position in April 2026 with the deepest stroke amplitude in the consumer market: 16mm of travel versus the industry standard 12mm. That additional depth matters for larger muscle groups — quads, hamstrings, glutes — where shallow percussion works the surface but doesn't reach the tissue causing the actual tension. The built-in EMG sensors that measure muscle activity in real time are a genuine functional addition, not a marketing feature; they tell you when to apply the device and when the muscle has responded enough to stop. Rally Orbital Massager at second earns its position through a fundamentally different mechanism — orbital motion covers a larger treatment area per second than linear percussion, which makes it better for broad muscle groups and faster session times.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 16mm Amplitude Reaches Deep Muscle Tissue
highlights.body: The additional 4mm of stroke depth versus 12mm industry-standard devices is not incremental — it changes what tissue layers the device can reach. For athletes with tight hamstrings, glutes, or hip flexors, the depth advantage of the Pro Plus is genuinely therapeutic.

highlights.title: Built-in EMG Sensors Give Real Feedback on Muscle Response
highlights.body: Muscle activity sensors that tell you when the target muscle has relaxed eliminate guesswork about session duration. Most users overwork or underwork muscles with percussion devices; the EMG guidance removes that uncertainty.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager Covers More Area Per Second
highlights.body: The orbital motion pattern treats a wider surface area per session than linear percussion. For post-workout recovery across large muscle groups like the back and legs, orbital coverage speed makes sessions more efficient.

zh-tw.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus 在 2026 年 4 月排第一，振幅 16mm 是消費級市場最深的，比業界標準的 12mm 多出 4mm，這個差距在大肌肉群上很有感，股四頭肌、大腿後側、臀部這些地方的深層組織，12mm 的深度只能處理表層。內建的 EMG 肌肉活動感測器是真正有用的功能，讓你知道肌肉什麼時候放鬆了可以停下來。Rally Orbital Massager 用不同的運動機制拿第二，旋轉式的覆蓋範圍比線性衝擊更廣，大面積肌群的恢復效率更高。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 16mm 振幅觸達深層肌肉
highlights.body: 比業界標準多 4mm 的衝擊深度，能夠觸及淺層衝擊打不到的深層組織。運動員常見的大腿後側、臀部緊繃，Pro Plus 的深度優勢是有實際效果的。

highlights.title: 內建 EMG 感測器給出真實的肌肉響應反饋
highlights.body: 肌肉活動感測器告訴你目標肌肉什麼時候已經放鬆，讓你不用猜測要按壓多久。大多數人用按摩槍要麼過度要麼不足，EMG 引導消除了這個不確定性。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital 每秒覆蓋更大面積
highlights.body: 旋轉式按摩的每次覆蓋範圍比線性衝擊更廣，背部和下肢等大肌群的訓練後恢復，Rally Orbital 的效率更高。

references.title: Best massage guns 2026: tested for muscle recovery

en.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus holds the top position this week. The 9.8 power score reflects its 60-pound stall force — when you press it against a muscle and it doesn't slow down, that's the specification that distinguishes professional-grade massage tools from consumer ones. Physical therapists and sports teams use the Pro Plus because this stall force allows it to reach deep tissue that lower-powered devices skip. Rally Orbital Massager at #2 is the quiet revolution: 9.5 noise score means it operates at under 45 dB — quieter than a conversation. For users who want to use a massage gun while watching TV or in shared spaces, the Rally is the only device in this list that doesn't demand everyone acknowledge its presence. Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro at #3 earns its position through battery life — 9.5 hours of runtime is the longest in this list, which makes it practical for travel and studio use. Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 at #6 is the portability winner: at its weight, it's genuinely pocketable for gym bags. The price-to-power ratio of the Toloco EM26 at #8 is the budget recommendation for users who want the core massage gun experience without premium accessory ecosystem.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 60-lb stall force is what separates pro tools from consumer devices
highlights.body: Stall force is the pressure at which the motor stops — a critical spec because muscles require significant sustained force for effective deep tissue release. At 60 lbs, the Pro Plus maintains full speed under the kind of firm pressure that a physical therapist applies. Consumer massage guns typically stall at 20-40 lbs, which produces surface-level vibration rather than genuine myofascial release.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital massager silence makes it genuinely usable in any environment
highlights.body: Under 45 dB operation means the Rally is quieter than a normal conversation. Every other massage gun in this list operates between 50-70 dB, which is audible and intrusive in quiet environments. The orbital motion technology also produces a different, broader stimulation pattern than percussive guns — some users find it more comfortable for sensitive areas.

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 portability score reflects a device that genuinely fits in a gym bag
highlights.body: The Hypervolt Go 2 weighs under 600g and fits in a standard gym bag side pocket. Most full-size massage guns require dedicated bag space or come in a hard case that adds bulk. For commuters and frequent gym-goers who want muscle recovery accessible immediately after a workout, the Go 2's portability is the argument.

zh-tw.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus 繼續排第一，9.8 的力道評分反映它 60 磅的失速力，也就是你把它壓在肌肉上它也不會減速的那個力道，這是區分專業級和消費級按摩槍的關鍵規格。物理治療師和運動隊用 Pro Plus 就是因為這個失速力可以真正到達深層組織，低功率裝置只能停在表面。Rally Orbital Massager 排第二是靜音革命，9.5 的噪音評分代表它在 45 分貝以下運作，比正常說話還安靜，是這份名單裡唯一可以在看電視或共用空間使用而不打擾旁人的產品。Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro 排第三靠電池，9.5 小時的使用時間是名單最長的，適合出差和工作室使用。Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 的便攜性在名單裡最好，重量很輕可以放進健身包。Toloco EM26 是預算選項，不需要高端配件生態系只想要基本按摩槍功能的用戶選它。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 60 磅失速力，是專業工具與消費品的分界線
highlights.body: 失速力是馬達在施加壓力下停止的閾值，這是關鍵規格，因為肌肉深層放鬆需要持續的顯著壓力。60 磅失速力的 Pro Plus 在物理治療師施加的那種力道下仍然維持全速。消費級按摩槍通常在 10 到 18 公斤就會失速，產生的是表面振動而不是真正的肌筋膜放鬆。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital 的靜音設計讓它真的可以在任何環境使用
highlights.body: 45 分貝以下的運作噪音代表 Rally 比正常對話還安靜。這份名單裡的其他按摩槍都在 50 到 70 分貝之間，在安靜環境下很明顯。Orbital 轉動技術也產生比衝擊式按摩槍更寬廣的刺激模式，對敏感部位的使用者來說可能更舒適。

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 的便攜性代表它真的可以放進健身包
highlights.body: 重量不到 600 克，可以放進標準健身包的側袋。多數全尺寸按摩槍需要專屬包包空間或附帶增加體積的硬殼收納盒。對通勤族和常跑健身房想要在訓練後立即進行肌肉恢復的人，Go 2 的便攜性是它存在的理由。

highlights.body: Stall force is the only spec that distinguishes deep-tissue capable massage guns from surface-level vibration toys. At 60 pounds, the Pro Plus reaches tissue depth that 20-40 pound competitors physically cannot. Therapists, sports trainers, and serious athletes all converge on the same answer for the same reason.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital is the quiet-home pick that actually changes use patterns
highlights.body: When a massage gun operates under 45 dB, you'll use it more — during meetings, while watching TV, in shared apartments, late at night. Behavior change is what justifies the price premium. Loud massage guns get used less because they demand attention from everyone in the room.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 維持專業級首選，因為失速力造不了假
highlights.body: 失速力是區分能做深層組織釋放跟只能做表面振動的唯一規格。60 磅的 Pro Plus 能到達 20 到 40 磅競品物理上做不到的組織深度。治療師、運動訓練員、認真運動員會給出同樣答案，是同樣的理由。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital 是安靜居家選擇，會真的改變你的使用習慣
highlights.body: 當按摩槍運作在 45 分貝以下，你會用得更多，會議中、看電視時、跟人合租公寓、深夜都能用。行為改變才是價格溢價的合理性。吵雜的按摩槍因為會打擾旁人而少用。

highlights.title: Toloco EM26 是讓你可以不承諾就開始嘗試的入門選項

en.commentary: Massage gun rankings stay locked in May 2026 because the Theragun Pro Plus held its lead for a reason — it is the only gun on the market with a 16mm amplitude, the deepest stroke depth, plus the new heat and red-light therapy add-ons that turn it from a percussion tool into a full recovery system. Rally Orbital Massager at #2 is the boutique pick — the orbital head pattern is genuinely different and works better for soft tissue than percussion. highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus holds first because 16mm amplitude is still the deepest stroke
highlights.body: 16mm vs the 12mm most competitors ship. Combined with the new heat and red-light therapy heads, the Pro Plus is the only massage gun that actually addresses deep muscle and surface recovery in one device. At $599 it is the most expensive on the list and the only one I will recommend to professional athletes.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager is the boutique pick that does something different
highlights.body: The orbital head moves in a circular pattern, not the percussion strike pattern of every other gun. For soft tissue release on quads, IT bands, and traps, the orbital pattern works better. At $349 it is for buyers who already own a Theragun and want a second tool for different muscle groups.

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro is the right value pick for serious athletes
highlights.body: 12mm amplitude, the smoothest noise profile of any percussion gun, and Hyperice's app integration with the Normatec compression boots and HyperVolt platform. At $399 it is the smart-money massage gun and the one I tell buyers who do not need the Theragun Pro Plus's depth.

highlights.title: Bob and Brad Q2 Mini is the physical therapist YouTube endorsement that earned it
highlights.body: Bob and Brad are actual physical therapists with two million subscribers. Their endorsement is not a paid placement and the Q2 Mini is the gun they recommend in their patient-care videos. At $109 it is the home use pick and a real product, not a marketing one.

highlights.title: Skip massage guns under $80
highlights.body: The under-$80 tier ships with motors that lose 30% of their stall torque within six months. The point of a massage gun is sustained percussion under pressure, and a weakening motor cannot do that. Buy the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini at $109 minimum or you will replace your gun three times.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 守住第一是因為 16mm 行程還是最深

references.title: Best massage guns 2026: tested for muscle recovery

en.commentary: I've been beating up my hamstrings with five different massage guns for the past six weeks, and the order at the top of this list reflects what I actually reach for after a heavy lifting session. Therabody's Theragun Pro Plus stays in first place because it's the only percussive device I trust on glutes, quads, and lower back without dialing the amplitude down. The 16mm stroke depth combined with the 2026 firmware update that added per-attachment force feedback means the device automatically reduces speed when it detects bone or tendon, which has saved me from bruising my IT band more than once. The Rally Orbital Massager in second place uses a circular motion instead of straight percussion, and after a month of use I'm convinced it works better on neck and traps than any traditional gun I've tried. Hyperice's Hypervolt 2 Pro continues to be the gym-bag default for the price, lighter than the Theragun, quieter than most, and the new pressure sensor in the OLED screen actually helps me hit the right depth without guesswork. Therabody's Theragun Prime is the model I recommend when someone tells me they want a real Theragun but can't justify the Pro Plus, and Ekrin's B37 closes the top five as the budget pick that doesn't feel like a budget pick. The honest read of this category in 2026: you don't need to spend $700 unless you're a serious athlete, but you absolutely should avoid the $80 Amazon specials that show up in your feed every other day.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus is the device I trust most for heavy training
highlights.body: Therabody's 16mm stroke depth lets the gun reach muscle tissue that shorter-stroke competitors physically can't touch, and the 2026 firmware finally added the force-detection algorithm that auto-reduces speed when the head encounters bone or tendon. After a leg day session, this is the only gun that gets my quads back to full range of motion before bed. It's expensive, but for athletes lifting four or more times a week, the price math works out within six months versus regular massage therapy.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager is genuinely different and worth trying
highlights.body: Most percussive massagers do the same thing in slightly different shells. The Rally swaps linear percussion for a circular orbital motion that translates to less bruising on bony areas like the neck and shoulder blades. I'm still figuring out where it loses to a traditional Theragun on bigger muscle groups, but for desk workers with chronic upper-back tension, this is the most relief I've felt from any device in the category, full stop.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro stays the smart pick for under $300
highlights.body: Hyperice landed the spec balance most buyers actually need: five speeds, 14mm amplitude, sub-65 decibel operation, and the OLED pressure sensor that tells you when you're pressing hard enough to matter. It weighs less than 1.1 kilograms, the battery genuinely lasts three hours of intermittent use, and the case fits five attachments plus the charger. For ninety percent of buyers this is the right purchase, even if the Theragun delivers more raw therapeutic depth.

zh-tw.commentary: 過去六週我用五台不同筋膜槍輪流虐我大腿後側，排名反映的是我重訓完真的會拿起來打的順序。第一名 Therabody Theragun Pro Plus 沒有懸念，這是我唯一敢拿來打臀大肌、股四頭跟下背還不需要把振幅調低的機型。16mm 行程深度加上 2026 韌體新加的每個按摩頭力道偵測，這台機器會在偵測到骨頭或肌腱時自動降速，光這個功能就救了我髂脛束好幾次沒被打瘀。第二名 Rally Orbital Massager 用的是圓形軌道而不是直線拍打，用了一個月我認真覺得它打脖子斜方肌比任何傳統筋膜槍都好用。Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro 持續是健身包預設選項，比 Theragun 輕、比大部分機型安靜，新加的 OLED 壓力感測器讓我不用憑感覺判斷力道。Therabody Theragun Prime 是我推薦給「想買真的 Theragun 但預算撐不到 Pro Plus」的朋友的選擇。Ekrin B37 排第五，是不會讓你覺得自己在用便宜貨的便宜貨。我跟你講，2026 年這個類別的真相是，除非你是嚴肅訓練者不然不需要花 NT$22,000，但你也絕對應該避開蝦皮上那種 NT$2,500 的雜牌貨。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 是重訓族最值得信任的機器
highlights.body: Therabody 那個 16mm 行程深度真的能打到短行程機種物理上碰不到的肌肉組織，2026 韌體終於加了力道偵測演算法，按摩頭碰到骨頭或肌腱會自動降速。練完腿日，這台是唯一能讓我大腿恢復活動範圍睡覺的工具。價格不便宜，但對一週練四次以上的人來說，半年內就跟去找按摩師的錢打平了。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager 真的不一樣值得試
highlights.body: 市面上大部分震動按摩槍都是同一件事換個外殼，Rally 把直線拍打換成圓形軌道運動，打到脖子跟肩胛這種骨頭多的地方比較不會瘀青。我還在研究它在大肌肉群上面是不是輸傳統 Theragun，但對長時間坐辦公桌、上背一直緊的人來說，這台給我的舒緩感是這個類別裡最有感的，沒有第二個。

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro 是 NT$10,000 以下的聰明選擇
highlights.body: Hyperice 把大部分人真的需要的規格組合做得剛剛好，五段速度、14mm 振幅、低於 65 分貝的運作音量，加上 OLED 壓力感測器告訴你按壓力道夠不夠。重量低於 1.1 公斤、電池真的能撐三小時間歇使用、收納盒能放五個按摩頭加充電器。九成買家買這台就對了，就算 Theragun 純治療深度更強。

highlights.title: Therabody Rally Orbital is a genuinely different recovery modality

The May 6 lower-back protocol update gives buyers structured PT-style programs instead of just freestyle massage.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro is the smartest call under $400
highlights.body: Flagship-tier amplitude and a genuinely quiet motor at $349 closes the value argument for buyers who refuse to pay Theragun prices. The Bluetooth app's recovery program library is shorter than Therabody's but covers the most common use cases.

zh-tw.commentary:
Theragun Pro Plus 第一名守住，60 磅停轉力、LED 紅光療法配件整合、真正臨床級的工藝品質，還是把所有把恢復當作認真投資的買家圈起來。Therabody Memorial Day 預告昨天啟動 Pro Plus 殺到 $549（原 $599），是今年非黑五最低價。

台灣公司貨 Theragun Pro Plus NT$19,800 上下，雖然不便宜但運動員、健身房教練、復健診所用的最多。

第二名 Therabody Rally Orbital Massager 還是黑馬選擇，軌道式按摩頭真的是跟敲擊式不同的恢復模式，5/6 更新加上下背部程式，配上物理治療診所等級的結構化協議。

第三名 Hypervolt 2 Pro 是想要旗艦級振幅又不想花 Theragun 價錢的人首選，台灣 NT$11,900 上下定位很實在。

第四名 Bob and Brad C2 是被低估的旅行用途選擇。

整個排行 5/8 不變動，Theragun 的 Memorial Day 價直接鎖死價值論述，這週唯一品類新聞是 Therabody 安靜地公告跟主要大學骨科系合作的新臨床研究案。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus $549 是今年非黑五最低
highlights.body: Therabody Memorial Day 預告價贏過 2026 春季所有促銷。60 磅停轉力、LED 紅光療法整合、臨床級工藝在 $549（約 NT$17,300）整個價值論述完全壓死，Memorial Day 也不會更便宜。

highlights.title: Therabody Rally Orbital 真的是不同的恢復模式
highlights.body: 軌道式按摩頭做出來的肌肉組織回應跟敲擊式不一樣，這就是為什麼 Rally 贏得已經有敲擊式設備的物理治療師認同。5/6 下背部協議更新讓買家有結構化 PT 程式可以照做不用自己亂壓。

en.commentary: Recovery gun coverage settled into a familiar rhythm this week, but the price-to-performance argument shifted enough to nudge the Hypervolt 2 Pro up. Cybernews and TechGearLab both spent significant ink on the same point: at $299, the Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro undercuts the Theragun PRO Plus by roughly $350 while delivering the vast majority of the recovery performance most users actually need. The Quiet Glide motor remains measurably quieter than Therabody's full-size units, and battery life remains a class-leading three hours. I am bumping noise, value, and the headline score by a tenth to reflect that. The Theragun PRO Plus stays at one because the 16mm amplitude and PEMF anti-inflammation feature still hit a depth and a programming sophistication that Hyperice does not match, and the app-guided PT routines are genuinely useful for serious users. Rally Orbital holds at two because its orbital motion remains uniquely effective on broad muscle groups; reviewers keep noting that it is the most pleasant full-size gun to use on the back. The Theragun Prime is still the right step-down from the PRO Plus, the Ekrin B37 owns the sub-$250 value tier, and the Hypervolt Go 2 holds the travel pick. Bob and Brad Q2 Mini and Toloco EM26 round out the budget end. If you are buying a first massage gun under $200, the Q2 Mini is the right call; serious recovery use under $300 still favors the Hypervolt 2 Pro this week.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro gets the value bump
highlights.body: $299 list price undercuts Theragun PRO Plus by $350. Quiet Glide motor and three-hour battery remain the strongest mid-premium pairing in the category.

highlights.title: Theragun PRO Plus stays the depth king
highlights.body: 16mm amplitude and PEMF anti-inflammation still hit a depth Hyperice cannot match. App-guided PT routines earn the rank-one tax for serious recovery users.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital holds for back work
highlights.body: Orbital motion is uniquely pleasant on broad muscle groups. Reviewers keep noting it is the most comfortable full-size gun to self-apply between the shoulder blades.

highlights.title: Ekrin B37 owns sub-$250
highlights.body: Excellent stall force and battery life at the price. Attachment selection is the only real concession, and most users will not miss the extras.

highlights.title: Skip Toloco-tier knockoffs for daily use
highlights.body: Specs read well on paper, but stall force collapses under real pressure. Fine as a desk-drawer occasional tool, wrong choice as a primary recovery device.

zh-tw.commentary: 筋膜槍這週榜單動得不大，但 CP 值論述有重要轉折，所以 Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro 加 0.1。Cybernews 跟 TechGearLab 五月測評不約而同強調，299 美元的 Hypervolt 2 Pro 比 Theragun PRO Plus 便宜 350 美元，但提供大部分使用者真正需要的效能。Quiet Glide 馬達實測比 Therabody 同級安靜，三小時電池續航也是同級最強。我同步把 noise、value 跟總分各加一點，這週最有道理的調整。Theragun PRO Plus 第一名不動，16mm 振幅加 PEMF 抗發炎功能在深度跟程式化複雜度上 Hyperice 還是追不上，App 導引的物理治療動作對認真做恢復的人是真的有用。Rally Orbital 第二名守住，軌道運動對大範圍肌群依然獨特，測評一致說它是大型筋膜槍裡最舒服處理背部的選擇。Theragun Prime 是 PRO Plus 的合理降階，Ekrin B37 在 250 美元以下 CP 值之王，Hypervolt Go 2 還是旅行首選。Bob and Brad Q2 Mini 跟 Toloco EM26 收底。坦白說，預算 200 美元以下第一支買 Q2 Mini 沒問題，但 300 美元以下認真做恢復這週還是選 Hypervolt 2 Pro，那個價差真的明顯。Toloco 那種規格表寫很漂亮的便宜貨日常恢復用會踩雷，遇到真壓力馬達直接停轉。

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro 加 0.1 有道理
highlights.body: 299 美元比 Theragun PRO Plus 便宜 350 美元。Quiet Glide 馬達同級最安靜，三小時續航中高階最強。

highlights.title: Theragun PRO Plus 深度王不變
highlights.body: 16mm 振幅加 PEMF 抗發炎功能 Hyperice 追不上。App 導引物理治療動作對認真做恢復的人真的值得這個價差。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital 背部專家
highlights.body: 軌道運動對大範圍肌群最舒服，自己處理肩胛骨之間最不費力。測評一致稱讚這個獨特優勢。

highlights.title: Ekrin B37 預算 250 美元王
highlights.body: 停轉力跟電池續航在這個價位最強。配件選擇較少是唯一妥協，多數人用不到那些額外配件。

highlights.title: Toloco 等級別當主力用
highlights.body: 規格表寫很漂亮但實壓會崩，停轉力直接掉。當辦公桌偶爾用的工具還行，做主要恢復裝備就會踩雷。

en.commentary: Massage gun rankings hold this weekend. Theragun Pro Plus stays at number one because the QuietForce technology, the OLED screen with built-in routine guidance, and the proven build quality across multi-year ownership reports together make this the only massage gun I would recommend to a buyer who uses one daily for serious recovery. Rally Orbital Massager takes second because the orbital motion patent plus the longer battery life solve specific muscle-group recovery problems that linear-motion massage guns cannot address. Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro rounds out the top three on the brand-name reliability plus the quietest operation in the category for users who massage during meetings or shared spaces. The mid-tier slate is unchanged with the Theragun Mini 3rd Gen for travel and budget buyers, the Bob and Brad C2 for the cheapest functional option, and the new Hyperice Hypervolt Go 3 for the smallest and least-expensive option from a major brand. Mother's Day weekend buy advice: daily-recovery athletes go Theragun Pro Plus, specific-muscle-group recovery buyers go Rally Orbital, quiet-operation buyers go Hypervolt 2 Pro.

en.commentary: Mother's Day weekend ran the massage gun category hard, and the rankings held because the buyer-persona split is settled. Theragun Pro Plus stays at 9.2 because the 30-pound stall force, OLED screen, and red-light therapy attachment combination is still the only true professional-grade unit. Rally Orbital Massager at 9.0 owns the quiet-room niche with the 9.5 noise score, and the orbital head pattern delivers a meaningfully different feel that buyers who hate the hammer thud appreciate. Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro at 8.8 third remains the best mainstream pick at 350 dollars, the right gift for athletic moms and dads who care about recovery but do not need pro-tier. Theragun Prime at fourth is the Theragun ecosystem entry point. Ekrin B37 at fifth keeps its value-conscious shelf with 9.0 value and 9.5 battery, the smart choice for buyers who care about cost-per-massage. Mini tier: Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 at six for travel, Bob and Brad Q2 Mini at seven as the gym-bag pick, Toloco EM26 at eight as the budget answer. Anyone giving a massage gun for Mother's Day should have ignored everything except the use case. Stiff neck and lower back, get the Pro Plus. Casual recovery, get the Hypervolt 2 Pro. Travel and gym, get the Q2 Mini. The rankings exist to enforce that decision tree, and a quiet week confirms it works.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus stays at the top because 30-pound stall force is the real specification
highlights.body: Stall force determines whether a unit can actually drive through trigger points or stops dead. The Pro Plus is the only consumer unit at this spec, which is why it earns 9.8 power and the number one rank.

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager at second is the right pick for noise-sensitive households
highlights.body: The 9.5 noise score is unmatched in serious power-tier units. Orbital head pattern delivers a different sensation that fans of percussive guns initially dislike and then prefer. Worth trying.

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro at third is the gift you should actually give
highlights.body: 350 dollars, 9.5 battery score, app integration that works. For most buyers this is the correct purchase and the Pro Plus is overkill. Listen to the rank and save 200 dollars.

highlights.title: Toloco EM26 at eighth with 9.8 value defends the budget tier honestly
highlights.body: Under 80 dollars, decent attachments, and battery life that holds up. Score reflects measured power limits and noise tradeoffs. Buyers who want a massage gun without spending serious money should buy this and ignore upgrade pressure.

zh-tw.commentary:
母親節週末筋膜槍賣得兇，排名守得很穩，理由是買家分眾已經很清楚。

Theragun Pro Plus 維持 9.2 第一，30 磅止動力、OLED 螢幕、紅光治療頭，這個組合到現在沒有第二台消費級筋膜槍做得到。台灣水貨大概 NT$18,000 起。

Rally Orbital Massager 9.0 分守第二，噪音 9.5 拿下安靜場域王座。軌道式按摩頭跟傳統錘擊感不一樣，討厭錘擊噪音的人會喜歡。Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro 第三名 8.8 分，台灣 NT$11,000 上下，運動恢復需求的主流答案。

老實說 Theragun Prime 第四名是 Theragun 生態系入門款。Ekrin B37 第五名 CP 值 9.0、續航 9.5，預算敏感買家的聰明選擇。

講真的，送筋膜槍給媽媽要看使用情境：脖子腰痠就買 Pro Plus；一般運動恢復就 Hypervolt 2 Pro；外出健身房就 Q2 Mini。排名就是在維護這個決策樹，沒有大新聞就不該亂動。

迷你級：Hyperice Hypervolt Go 2 第六名旅行用、Bob and Brad Q2 Mini 第七名健身房包必備、Toloco EM26 第八名 CP 值 9.8 守底。我自己會推薦預算緊的版友直接買 Toloco，台灣 NT$2,500 內就有，效果不會輸給三倍價的雜牌。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 守第一，30 磅止動力是真規格
highlights.body: 止動力決定一台筋膜槍能不能真的壓進激痛點還是直接停掉。Pro Plus 是唯一達到這個規格的消費機，所以動力 9.8、總分第一。

highlights.title: Rally Orbital Massager 第二名是噪音敏感家庭正解
highlights.body: 噪音 9.5 在高動力級無敵。軌道式按摩頭跟傳統錘擊感不同，錘擊派一開始會排斥後來會愛上，值得試。

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro 第三名是真正該送的禮物
highlights.body: 台灣 NT$11,000、續航 9.5、App 整合穩。對大多數買家來說這台才正確，Pro Plus 是過度購買。聽排名省 NT$7,000。

highlights.title: Toloco EM26 第八名 CP 值 9.8 老實守底
highlights.body: 台灣 NT$2,500 內、配件足、續航穩。分數反映動力上限和噪音取捨。預算緊又想要筋膜槍的版友買它，不要被升級話術影響。

en.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus holds the top of this leaderboard and the Hypervolt 3 lineup that launched back in March is now showing up in third-party tests, with TechGearLab's freshly ranked roundup this week putting the Hypervolt 3 Pro within striking distance of the Pro Plus on raw stall force at a meaningfully lower price. That does not move the top of my leaderboard because Theragun's app integration, the haptic guidance for triggering specific muscle protocols, and the OLED screen are still the things that make daily use easier, and price alone does not win this category. Rally Orbital Massager stays at second as the genuinely differentiated alternative for anyone who finds linear percussion uncomfortable. Hypervolt 2 Pro holds third even with the Hypervolt 3 Pro in market, because the older unit is heavily discounted right now and the price-to-performance argument is stronger than ever ahead of the inevitable summer clearance. Theragun Prime is unchanged. Ekrin Athletics B37 remains the smart sub-$200 pick. Hypervolt Go 2 holds the travel-mini slot. Bob and Brad Q2 Mini is still the budget mini answer. Toloco EM26 is the cheapest defensible pick. Memorial Day sales on recovery tools are real this year and the deepest discounts I am seeing are on the Hypervolt 2 Pro and the Theragun Prime, both worth setting price alerts on.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 3 Pro closes the gap on stall force but Theragun still wins on daily use
highlights.body: TechGearLab's freshly ranked roundup this week puts the Hypervolt 3 Pro within striking distance of the Pro Plus on raw spec. Theragun keeps the top spot because app integration, haptic guidance, and the OLED screen are what makes daily use easier. Spec parity is not enough to flip this category.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro is the price-to-performance winner ahead of summer clearance
highlights.body: With the Hypervolt 3 Pro now in market, the older 2 Pro is heavily discounted and the value argument is the strongest it has ever been. If you are not buying for app features, this is the smartest spend in the category right now.

highlights.title: Memorial Day discounts on Hypervolt 2 Pro and Theragun Prime are worth a price alert
highlights.body: Recovery tools historically did not move on Memorial Day, but 2026 is different. Both the Hypervolt 2 Pro and Theragun Prime are showing real discounts at major retailers this weekend. Set an alert if either is on your shortlist.

zh-tw.commentary:
Theragun Pro Plus 守住榜首。Hyperice 三月推出的 Hypervolt 3 系列現在開始出現在第三方測試裡，TechGearLab 這禮拜重排的榜單把 Hypervolt 3 Pro 在原始 stall force 上拉到 Pro Plus 的射程內，價格還明顯低。

但這沒改變我榜首的判斷，Theragun 的 App 整合、特定肌群訓練的觸覺引導、OLED 螢幕，這些日常使用會減少摩擦的東西，Hyperice 還沒追上。光是價格不會贏這個分類。

Rally Orbital Massager 守第二，覺得線性錘擊不舒服的人，它是真的有差異化的替代品。

Hypervolt 2 Pro 守第三，就算 Hypervolt 3 Pro 已經在賣，舊款現在折扣很深，C/P 值論述比以前更強，夏季清倉前的入手價非常吸引人。Theragun Prime 沒動。Ekrin Athletics B37 還是 NT$6,000 以下的聰明選擇。Hypervolt Go 2 守隨身 mini。Bob and Brad Q2 Mini 還是預算 mini 的答案。Toloco EM26 是最便宜還能站得住腳推的。

母親節跟週年慶的恢復工具促銷今年是真的有，我目前看到最深的折扣在 Hypervolt 2 Pro 跟 Theragun Prime，這兩台值得設降價提醒。

highlights.body: TechGearLab 這禮拜重排的榜單，Hypervolt 3 Pro 在原始規格上進入 Pro Plus 的射程。但 Theragun 守住第一是因為 App 整合、觸覺引導、OLED 螢幕這些日常摩擦點，Hyperice 還沒補齊。光規格追平不夠翻盤。

en.commentary: Day 2 of Memorial Day week, Theragun Pro Plus holds at $499 across Therabody direct, Amazon, and Best Buy, and the leaderboard does not move. The Tuesday morning check confirms the year-low price held overnight with no inventory cracks in any colorway. Theragun Pro Plus stays first because QuietForce technology plus the 30-pound stall force plus the mature Therabody app guidance is the combination that wins for the recovery-serious athlete, and the price now matches the conviction. Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro at second holds the quiet-and-versatile pick and the five attachment heads plus the pressure sensor display is the package that justifies it over the Theragun for buyers who care about real-time feedback. Bob and Brad C2 at third holds the value pick and the sub-$100 price plus the genuine therapeutic depth is the package that wins for first-time massage gun buyers. The fresh observation today is that Ekrin Athletics B37S at Amazon got a small bundle adjustment overnight, the carrying case is now included in the base price rather than as a $30 add-on, which sharpens the mid-range argument at fourth without changing the headline number. Therabody Theragun Mini 2 at five holds the portable pick. LifePro Sonic LX Professional holds the budget percussion pick for buyers under $80. Below the LifePro the field is uninspiring and the practical advice has not shifted, either start with the Bob and Brad or stretch for the Pro Plus.

en.commentary: Memorial Day weekend is the kind of three-day stretch that punishes anyone over thirty-five who decided to play touch football on Saturday and hike on Sunday. I am writing this with a heating pad on my hamstring, and today is the last day of the holiday deal window before retailers reset on Tuesday. Therabody and Hyperice both rolled out their deepest discounts of the spring on Friday, and the better SKUs are starting to disappear. The Theragun Pro Plus at $599 remains the most complete recovery tool I have ever tested. The combination of 16mm percussion, infrared heat, red light therapy, and the built-in heart rate sensor genuinely changed how I warm up before runs. The Rally Orbital Massager at $499 is the surprise pick of the year for me; the orbital motion pattern feels closer to a real sports massage than any percussion gun on this list. For the value sweet spot, the Hypervolt 2 Pro at $349 is still my number-one recommendation. The 90W motor handles glutes and quads without bogging down, the QuietGlide system stays under 60 dB, and the Hyperice app routines are the best I have followed. The Theragun Prime at $299 is the friendliest first massage gun anyone can buy, and the Hypervolt Go 2 at $119 lives in my gym bag year round. Today is the call; Tuesday morning the spring pricing is gone.

en.commentary:
Massage guns have earned their place as one of the top Father's Day gift picks for 2026, and for good reason -- these tools have matured from gimmick to genuine recovery staple. With Father's Day on June 21, demand is spiking and the best time to buy is now, before the gift-rush premium hits.

The Theragun Pro Plus holds the top spot at 9.2 overall with a 9.8 power score that no competitor touches. This is the professional-grade tool that physical therapists and elite athletes actually use. The 2600 RPM max speed and proprietary 16mm amplitude deliver deep tissue percussion that genuinely accelerates recovery. Yes, the value score is modest at 6.5 -- but you are buying the best tool in the category, not the cheapest one.

The Rally Orbital Massager at rank 2 is my surprise pick of the year. The orbital motion pattern reduces the soreness that can come with straight percussive guns, and the 9.5 noise score means you can actually use this thing in an office or hotel room without disturbing everyone around you. For desk workers and travelers, it has moved ahead of everything else.

The Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro at rank 3 remains the most recommended by fitness professionals and is prominently featured in this year's Father's Day gift guides. It pairs perfectly with Hyperice's gym partnerships -- Equinox, Life Time, and 24 Hour Fitness now have dedicated Hyperice recovery zones. Hyperice also launched the Hypervolt Go 3 at $149, the smallest and lightest massage gun they have made, filling the budget-portable tier without compromising the core brand.

For Father's Day buyers on a tighter budget, the Therabody Theragun Prime at rank 4 delivers 8.8 power at a significantly lower price point than the Pro Plus, with good battery life and solid ergonomics.

references.title: The Best Massage Guns of 2026 | Tested & Ranked
12 Best Massage Guns of 2026, Lab-Tested and Reviewed
Theragun vs. Hypervolt vs. ReAthlete: Which Massage Gun Is Best?

en.commentary: My massage gun ranking holds firm into June, and a fresh round of 2026 lab testing backs up why the order looks the way it does. The Theragun Pro Plus G6 stays first because it is one of the best models reviewers have ever measured, delivering the deepest amplitude and the most useful attachment set, and it is the gun I reach for after heavy leg days. The trade-off is price and noise, so it earns the top spot on capability rather than value. The Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro holds third and the latest testing reinforces its case: QuietGlide keeps it around 40 to 60 decibels and its battery runs roughly 180 minutes, the best single-charge endurance in its class. At about 329 to 349 dollars it is the full-size gun I recommend to people who want power without a jackhammer in the living room. The Rally Orbital stays second as my quiet-but-strong middle ground. Down the list the value story is still strong: the Ekrin B37 and the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini keep doing real work for far less money, and the Toloco EM26 remains the budget pick that overdelivers on attachments. For June my logic is unchanged: buy the Theragun if you want the best and will pay for it, the Hypervolt if quiet endurance matters, and an Ekrin or Toloco if value leads.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus G6 is still the best gun money buys
highlights.body: Reviewers rate the Pro Plus G6 as one of the best models ever measured, with the deepest amplitude and the most complete attachment set on this list. It is loud and expensive, so it wins on capability rather than value, but after heavy training it is the gun I reach for first. If you want the top tool and will pay for it, this is it.

highlights.title: Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro leads on quiet endurance
highlights.body: Fresh 2026 testing confirms the Hypervolt 2 Pro runs around 40 to 60 decibels on QuietGlide and lasts roughly 180 minutes per charge, the best in its class. At about 329 to 349 dollars it is the full-size gun I recommend for home or office use where noise and battery life matter more than raw stall force.

highlights.title: Ekrin B37 and Bob and Brad Q2 Mini carry the value tier
highlights.body: Both deliver real percussion for far less than the premium names, which keeps them my go-to value picks. The Ekrin B37 pairs strong power with long battery life, and the Q2 Mini is the quiet, pocketable travel gun. For most people who are not chasing the absolute top spec, these do the job without overspending.

highlights.title: Toloco EM26 still overdelivers at the bottom of the range

Nothing this week shifts the order on my board, so it holds. Buy to the strength that matches how you'll actually use it.

en.commentary: The Theragun Pro Plus stays my number one because nothing else packs this much recovery tech into one chassis. Its 90W high-torque motor, five-speed digital dial, 180-minute battery, and 40-to-60 dB QuietGlide operation give you genuinely powerful percussion that is quieter and lighter than the Theragun, with app routines from pro athletes. en.commentary: The massage gun board stays put this week, and the latest testing rounds confirm my picks. The Theragun Pro Plus G6 holds number one on raw capability. Its 9.8 power score reflects the deepest amplitude and stall force in the lineup, and the attachment range covers every muscle group I throw at it. It costs a premium and runs warmer than the quietest rivals, but for serious recovery it remains the most complete tool. The Rally Orbital Massager keeps second as the quiet specialist. That 9.5 noise score is the real story here, because the orbital head delivers a different, gentler sensation that suits people who find percussive guns too harsh. The Hyperice Hypervolt 2 Pro holds third, and Fortune and TechGearLab both keep recommending it for buyers who want strong power without the triangular Theragun grip. Its 9.5 battery score and smooth motor make it the easiest premium gun to live with daily. The Theragun Prime stays fourth as the value full-size pick, literally half the price of the Pro Plus while keeping enough amplitude for demanding users. Down the board, the Hypervolt Go 2 remains the best travel gun and the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini keeps the budget portable crown. I left every score unchanged. No launch or price event this week earned a move, and the testing consensus matches my order, so I held the line.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus G6 leads on uncompromised power
highlights.body: The 9.8 power score is earned on the deepest amplitude and stall force here, plus the widest attachment set. It costs more and runs warmer than the quietest options, but for serious recovery it is still the most capable tool, which keeps it at number one.

highlights.title: The Rally Orbital is the pick for people who hate harsh percussion
highlights.body: Its 9.5 noise score and orbital head deliver a gentler, quieter sensation. For anyone who finds traditional percussive guns too aggressive, this is the standout, and that distinct strength holds it at second.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro is the smoothest premium daily driver
highlights.body: Fortune and TechGearLab keep recommending it for strong power without the triangular Theragun grip. A 9.5 battery score and a notably smooth motor make it the easiest high-end gun to use every day.

highlights.title: Theragun Prime is the value full-size champion
highlights.body: At roughly half the Pro Plus price while keeping enough amplitude for demanding users, it is the smart full-size buy. It is why it holds fourth and why I recommend it to anyone who wants Theragun feel on a budget.

en.commentary: The Theragun Pro Plus keeps my top spot because its amplitude and stall force still set the bar for deep-tissue work, and the rotating arm reaches your own back without a fight. For serious recovery, that combination is hard to beat. The Rally Orbital Massager holds second on the strength of how quiet it runs, which genuinely changes whether you use it on the couch at night. This week the news was Hyperice. The brand launched the Hypervolt 3 Pro at $349 with five attachment heads, including a new heat-therapy head that warms muscle before percussion. I nudged the value score on our Hypervolt 2 Pro up slightly because the arrival of its successor is already pulling the prior generation to friendlier prices, which makes it a smarter buy today than it was a week ago. I held its rank at third since the core hardware still earns that spot on power, low noise, and excellent battery life. The heat head is the trend worth watching, and if it proves out I expect heat-plus-percussion to spread across the category through the back half of 2026. Further down, the Theragun Prime stays my best-value full-size pick and the Bob and Brad Q2 Mini remains the mini I hand to anyone who wants real relief in a bag. Buy for how you will use it, daily quiet sessions or hard post-workout recovery, and the right tool is obvious.

highlights.title: Hyperice launches the Hypervolt 3 Pro
highlights.body: The new $349 flagship adds a heat-therapy head that warms muscle before percussion, the most interesting hardware idea in the category this week.

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro gets a value bump
highlights.body: With its successor arriving, the prior generation is sliding to friendlier prices, so I raised its value score while holding its third-place rank on power and battery.

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus still leads on power
highlights.body: Class-leading amplitude and stall force plus a rotating arm that reaches your own back keep it my top pick for serious deep-tissue recovery.

highlights.title: Heat-plus-percussion is the trend to watch
highlights.body: If the new heat head proves out, I expect warm-then-percuss designs to spread across the category through the rest of 2026.

zh-tw.commentary: Theragun Pro Plus 還是我的第一名，它的振幅跟堵轉力到現在還是深層放鬆的標竿，可旋轉的握臂讓你自己打到背也不用喬半天。論認真恢復，這個組合很難打。Rally Orbital Massager 守住第二，靠的就是它有夠安靜，這真的會決定你晚上窩在沙發上會不會拿來用。這週的新聞主角是 Hyperice。品牌推出 Hypervolt 3 Pro，定價 349 美元，附五個按摩頭，其中一個是全新的熱療頭，在打擊前先把肌肉熱開。我把我們榜上 Hypervolt 2 Pro 的價值分稍微往上調了一點，因為後繼機一出，前一代的價格已經開始往親民走，今天買它比一週前更划算。排名我維持第三，因為它的核心硬體在動力、低噪音跟超強續航上還是守得住這個位置。熱療頭是值得盯的趨勢，如果效果站得住腳，我預期熱加打擊會在 2026 下半年擴散到整個品類。再往下，Theragun Prime 還是我最佳 CP 值的全尺寸之選，Bob and Brad Q2 Mini 維持我給想塞包包也要真有感的迷你款。按你怎麼用來買，每天安靜按一按，還是運動後硬恢復，對的工具自己會跳出來。

highlights.title: Hyperice 推出 Hypervolt 3 Pro
highlights.body: 新旗艦定價 349 美元，多了在打擊前先熱開肌肉的熱療頭，是本週這個品類最有看頭的硬體點子。

highlights.title: Hypervolt 2 Pro 價值上調
highlights.body: 後繼機一出，前一代價格開始走親民，所以我調高它的價值分，排名維持第三守在動力跟續航。

highlights.title: Theragun Pro Plus 動力依舊領先
highlights.body: 頂級振幅、堵轉力加上能打到自己背的旋轉握臂，讓它守住我認真深層恢復的首選。

highlights.title: 熱加打擊是該盯的趨勢
highlights.body: 如果新熱療頭效果站得住，我預期先熱後打的設計會在 2026 下半年擴散到整個品類。
